title: Object 13

{{DISPLAYTITLE:Object 13: Luck Clover}}__NOTOC__ {{CSS|User:Papashfh/Better Tabs.css|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Sulphur+Point&display=swap'); @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Libre+Barcode+128&display=swap'); .addendum { background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgb(92, 107, 92) 3px, rgb(113, 138, 113) 7px); border: 5px solid black; font-family: 'Sulphur Point'; color: white; margin: 5px; padding: 5px; background-size: 100% 7px; } }}
{{Audio|file=Saria's song.mp3|align=center|loop=yes|volume=0.05}}
==Description== {{Blurimage|file=Object 13 new.jpg|caption=Most popular image of Object 13, taken in a break room of [[Level 6.5]].}} Possibly the most powerful objects, '''Luck Clovers''' are rare items present within the Backrooms, assigned the number 13. This designation has often been called 'ironic,' due to the object being vastly different than what the number 13 often symbolizes in many cultures. While thirteen is the so-called 'unlucky number,' all instances of '''Object 13''' makes its possessor luckier. The database has been vandalized before to replace its numerical designation, often replacing it with [[Object 30|Retractable Entity Detectors]]. '''Object 13''' has garnered popularity due to its immensely powerful abilities, granting its possessor 'divine luck.' While it doesn't provide extreme advantages, it still has numerous benefits; such as the ability to easily save an individual's life. Furthermore, '''Object 13''' is able to adjust and adapt to any individual possessing it, generating a solution for any nearby issues. For example, if one were to get infected with [[Entity 8#Egremond Syndrome|Egremond Syndrome]] a cure would manifest in vicinity. Although curiously, every solution provides the object's user with high amounts of dopamine, which otherwise would not be expected in certain situations. This phenomenon has been widely studied, particularly in efforts to find a cure for many [[w:c:backrooms:Category:Diseases|diseases]]. Often, '''Object 13''''s solutions are analyzed by research teams from various organizations that then attempt to recreate them using similar chemical compounds. However, this has been deemed increasingly difficult, as a result of every solution disappearing 24 hours after its use. As of current, no experiments have been successful in their goal. '''Object 13''''s appearance comes in the form of a clover with emerald and golden borders, made out of jewels. These clovers come in the form of three leaves, and leaf is shaped like a heart. The clovers' dimensions are small to the size of pebbles. Furthermore, the shape of '''Object 13''' varies, sometimes having crystal-like leaves, and other times flat leaves; the color of the object also varies. === Luck Clover Juice === {{Blurimage|width=250px|align=left|caption=A bottle of Luck Clover Juice, taken in [[Level 4.3]].|file=Luck Clover Juice.jpg}} ''Luck Clover Juice'' is a variant of '''Object 13''', serving as its liquid form. It is an acid-green liquid in the form of a soda, usually appearing in glass bottles; its overall taste seems to be a blend of mud and lime. Research on this variant has found that ''Luck Clover Juice''''s effects are different from those of '''Object 13'''; where '''Object 13''' grants beneficial luck to any individual possessing it, ''Luck Clover Juice'' provides luck to any individual who drinks it. The variant's luck is temporary, typically lasting for 72 hours. First discovered within [[Level 476]], ''luck Clover Juice'' has been deemed more commonly found than its parent object. It was originally found by a wanderer unaffiliated with any organization, and since then has become very popular as '''Object 13''''s variant. Alongside [[Level 476]], ''Luck Clover Juice'' can be found most commonly where its parent object is found. No amount of research has determined how emerald materials could possibly be transformed into a green soda-like liquid, especially considering how the materials do not lose their pigment. As a result, it is speculated that the emerald which exists as part of ''Luck Clover Juice'' is actually a Backrooms-based material, rather than emerald from [[The Frontrooms]]. Furthermore, when ''Luck Clover Juice'' is evaporated, a strange, unidentified gas will appear. Its effects and colors are the same as the liquid's, and one can activate said benefits by breathing in the gas. ==Potential Hazards== While any distinct hazards of '''Object 13''' are yet to be identified, Dr. Robert D. Andrew, a researcher working with [[The K4|Kappa IV]], has suggested numerous perils the object potentially may exhibit. Several studies and experiments have been committed, ending in a confirmation of some of Robert's claims. The list of perils was originally published in 2012 and at first received negative feedback from the Backrooms public, for many thought up to that point that '''Object 13''' was incapable of doing harm or damage. The claims published by Dr. Andrew are listed below. {| class="article-table" style="border: 2px solid #138eba; margin:0;" |+ | Hazard | style="border-left:solid 2px #138eba" |Description | style="border-left:solid 2px #138eba" |Status |- | Dependency | style="border-left:solid 2px #138eba" |A possessor may get addicted to the dopamine provided by '''Object 13''''s solutions. This results in the forsaken individual abusing the object's abilities as much as they can, provided they receive the dopamine from solutions. This effectively puts them in danger. | style="border-left:solid 2px #138eba" |Confirmed; 2024 |- | Perception | style="border-left:solid 2px #138eba" |A solution may not be found, worsening the situation. This can happen from certain possible circumstances which have not yet been determined. | style="border-left:solid 2px #138eba" |Confirmed; 2014 |- | Finiteness | style="border-left:solid 2px #138eba" |The solutions '''Object 13''' generates and provides are limited. This may result in certain problems being solved while others are ignored and potentially worsened. | style="border-left:solid 2px #138eba" |Unconfirmed |- | Misinterpretation | style="border-left:solid 2px #138eba" |The object misunderstands the possessor's needs. Either the given solution does not help solve the issue, or it solves the issue but remains undesirable in the long term. | style="border-left:solid 2px #138eba" |False; 2019 |} ==Materialization== '''Object 13''' is typically found in levels that contain low amounts of hazards. They can be found easily, though in rare cases they seem hidden. The object was originally discovered within [[Level 180]], and since then it has been found in a variety of other levels. Despite research and studies, no patterns in its appearances, other than what levels it can be commonly found in, have been determined. ==Addendum== Below is an entry taken from the journal of a now-deceased wanderer. This sprung the idea of an all-powerful Luck Clover, able to solve the problem without generating one.
My Ender.


I wasn't expecting my life to be altered by something so small. Something seemingly so vulnerable. A clover. Golden borders, emerald finishing. It was as big as a pretty white pebble. It looks like a gem, something you would see in a jewelry store. Although nothing in its appearance suggested human crafting. I was never healthy. I had a terminal disease which would encompass me before I could become 15 years old. It loomed over me, and I was terrified. When the diagnosis was made, I couldn't understand what was happening. All I saw was my mother crying into my father's shoulders, as his face tried to not shed tears. I was the ripe age of eight. My body was small, my fingers small, my head small. And soon, the world was small. I had to be taken out of school due to my worsening condition. Nurses in scrubs were my caregivers; my clothes felt like paper; and I lived in a house that smelled of cleaning detergent. When I found myself in the Backrooms, my disease could only worsen. I wasn't able to find any medical professionals, and by the time I finally did, it seemed my life was already on the edge of a cliff. When I came across the clover, I ignored it at first. But I took it, hoping to bribe the doctor with the expensive jewelry into helping fix my treatment. When I took the object into my possession, the disease suddenly went away. It disappeared without a trace. This event came much later on, but during that time period, a lot of other problems were suddenly solved. As if they never existed. I didn't know what to do with the clover. Soon enough, all my wants were solved. Besides leaving my newfound home. I could help people with this power. But I know I am not the one who should hold that responsibility. Anything I'd ever want could be provided. But with that went my ability to live and experience life. I mastered it all. And now, I'm feeling like my life is over.
I just want to die.
